fields.title: Fix stale baseSystemPrompt caching in before_agent_start
fields.description:
The before_agent_start handler in src/services/prompt-modes.ts caches event.systemPrompt on the first call and reuses it for every subsequent call. If the user switches sessions/projects, or pi rebuilds the base prompt with different AGENTS.md/prompt-template content, the cached baseSystemPrompt becomes stale.

## Problem location
src/services/prompt-modes.ts, the before_agent_start handler (~line 298-307):
```typescript
if (baseSystemPrompt === undefined) baseSystemPrompt = event.systemPrompt;
```
This only captures the prompt once. All later calls reuse that stale value.

## Fix
Always update baseSystemPrompt from event.systemPrompt when the mode system prompt is NOT already appended. The tricky part: avoid double-appending the mode prompt if before_agent_start fires multiple times within the same agent turn.

One approach: instead of caching baseSystemPrompt forever, always use event.systemPrompt as the base and append the mode prompt. The comment says 'pi may call before_agent_start multiple times (e.g. model switches)' — so the concern is double-appending. A clean fix: strip any previously-appended mode prompt before re-appending, or use a marker/sentinel to detect whether the mode prompt is already present.

## Quality gate
Run `npm run gate` from extensions/tau/ before marking done.
fields.close_reason: Implemented strip-then-append mode prompt handling and added regression coverage for refreshed base system prompts.
